headline: What the Halal Dispute Is Actually About
context: Kelantan and Terengganu proposed halal standards exceeding JAKIM's requirements. The real fight: who controls a RM5.6B certification industry.

cards.big: The fight is not about food.
cards.sub: It's about which agency controls a RM5.6 billion industry.

cards.big: Halal certification is RM5.6B/year. Standards = market access.
cards.sub: When Kelantan proposed higher standards, the pushback was commercial.
Shafi'i jurisprudence permits state-level ijtihad. The constitution supports it.

cards.big: This mirrors five other federal-state conflicts right now.
Religion, business, jurisdiction — one inflammatory headline.
cards.sub: All three concerns valid. None about nasi lemak.

cards.big: Treating a jurisdictional question as a culture war risks Malaysia's real advantage: global halal credibility.
